# PostHTML

Give AI agents the ability to upload, edit, and share HTML posts programmatically via CLI or MCP.

## Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router, Drizzle ORM, Neon Postgres
- Better Auth (Google OAuth only) + `@better-auth/api-key` plugin
- CLI: `post` (`@androff/posthtml-cli`)
- MCP (Model Context Protocol): `/api/mcp` (header auth) or `/api/mcp/{token}` (URL auth)

## Key files
- `web/lib/auth.ts` — Better Auth config
- `web/lib/auth-client.ts` — auth client
- `web/db/schema.ts` — Drizzle schema
- `web/app/api/mcp/lib.ts` — shared MCP server logic
- `web/app/api/mcp/route.ts` — header-based MCP route (backward compat)
- `web/app/api/mcp/[token]/route.ts` — URL-based MCP proxy route
- `web/app/dashboard/components/McpSection.tsx` — MCP dashboard UI
- `web/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — dashboard (API keys, MCP, posts)
- `cli/src/index.ts` — CLI entrypoint (commander)

## Auth flow
1. User signs in with Google via landing page
2. Session cookie for dashboard
3. Dashboard generates API keys (prefix `post_`) or MCP tokens (prefix `mcp_`)
4. CLI uses `x-api-key` header → `auth.api.verifyApiKey()` on post routes

## MCP
- URL format: `https://posthtml.vercel.app/api/mcp/<mcp_...token>`
- Generate from dashboard sidebar → MCP Server → Generate URL
- No `x-api-key` header needed — auth is in the URL path
- Old `/api/mcp` with `x-api-key` header still works

## API auth
- Dashboard routes: session cookie
- Post/CLI routes: `x-api-key` header (verified via Better Auth `verifyApiKey`)
- MCP: `x-api-key` header (old) or URL path token (new)
- Public viewer `/p/:id`: no auth

## Installation

### CLI
```bash
npm install -g @androff/posthtml-cli
```

Configure your API key:
```bash
post setup --key <your-key>
# or set env vars:
export POST_API_KEY=***
export POST_URL=https://posthtml.vercel.app
```

### Commands
```bash
post upload index.html                              # upload a post
post upload index.html --data '{"status":"draft"}'   # upload + attach JSON data
post upload index.html --data-file meta.json         # or merge a whole JSON file
post upload index.html --private                     # create as private (owner-only)
post upload index.html --public                      # create as public (default)
post replace <id> file.html --private                # update + set private (preserves URL)
post replace <id> file.html --public                 # update + set public
post list                                            # list your posts
post delete <id>                                     # delete a post
post replace <id> file.html                          # update post content (preserves URL)
post data get <id>                                   # read a post's JSON data
post data set <id> --key <k> --value '<json>'        # merge one key into a post's JSON data
post data set <id> --file data.json                  # merge a whole JSON file into a post's data
```

## Quick test
```bash
POST_URL=https://posthtml.vercel.app POST_API_KEY=*** post list
```
